title: Shopping widget progress bar does not animate — stays full width until reset
description:
The auto-reset progress bar on the dashboard shopping widget is supposed to hold at full width for 30 seconds after a store pill is tapped, then shrink horizontally from 100% to 0% over the next 30 seconds. At 60 seconds total, the filter resets to 'All'.

Observed behavior (confirmed via timed screenshots on Mac):
- The progress bar IS visible as a thin colored line at the bottom of the widget
- The bar stays at full width for the entire 60-second duration
- The filter DOES reset after ~60 seconds (timer is working)
- The bar does NOT visually shrink at any point — no animation occurs
- Customer tested multiple times with consistent results

The timer logic fires correctly (the reset happens). The visual animation does not work.

Root cause investigation needed:
1. Add console.log or devtools breakpoint inside the `tick` function in `useAutoResetTimer` to confirm what `progress` state value is being set during the 30-60 second window
2. Check if React is actually re-rendering the progress bar div when progress changes
3. Check if the inline style `width: ${progress * 100}%` is updating in the DOM
4. Check if the CSS transition property (`progress === 1 ? 'none' : 'width 100ms linear'`) is interfering
5. Check if requestAnimationFrame loop is being cancelled prematurely by effect cleanup or data refetch re-renders

The code logic in useAutoResetTimer appears correct on inspection. This needs runtime debugging.

Acceptance criteria:
- Progress bar visually shrinks from full width to zero over the 30-60 second window
- Shrink animation is smooth and visible in dark mode
- Any interaction with the widget resets the bar to full width
- Bar disappears when filter resets to 'All'

Dev Lead.notes: James diagnosed and fixed both root causes. Clean.
